correspondence.text:
📫 Correspondence — Thursday, May 7, 2026

Good morning, Mister Lang. The wider world is, if I may say, engaged in some world-class fucking nonsense again, Sir — though I note with cautious interest that Washington and Tehran appear to be negotiating rather than escalating this morning, which passes for progress. The clouds over Edmonds are giving way to sun; high of 62°F today. Nothing of personal consequence arrived in overnight correspondence — a GitClear trial expiry notice (seven days remaining), a Carnival Cruise terms-and-conditions action item, and the customary commercial flotsam. Your Subaru is due for its 144,000-mile service, in case the calendar had not reminded you. Onwards, Sir.

weather: 51°F overcast at 5am, clouds giving way to sun today. High 62°F, light SSE winds. Low tonight 50°F. Tomorrow: mostly sunny, highs in the low 60s.

local_news.findings: Edmonds Jazz Connection May 9 (full-day event, young jazz musicians). SketcherFest Edmonds 2026 poster unveiled — artist David Chamness (Shoreline-based, retired architect) captured the 5th Ave/Main St roundabout. Edmonds Driftwood Players: 'The Mysterious Affair at Styles' (Agatha Christie) opens May 15–June 7, Wade James Theatre. Edmonds Center for the Arts: Wailin' Jennys May 16, Alan Doyle May 17. Cascadia Art Museum free admission 3rd Thursdays (next: May 21, 5pm tour). City accepting 2027 Lodging Tax Fund applications. Edmonds Food Bank: GiveBIG and Stamp Out Hunger campaigns active. Edmonds College Triton Visit Day: May 15, 2–5pm, free, register at outreach@edmonds.edu.
No new meeting since May 5 (Stephanie Lucash appointment covered in prior session). Next regular meeting TBD.
No new crime report for May 7. Edmonds crime grade A- current. Year-over-year: violent crime down 0.9%, property crime up 14.6%.

career.district_jobs: ⚠️ STANDING REMINDER: Mr. Lang's SPS interest card is miscategorized as Kitchen Assistant Manager — must be corrected. Indeed lists 145 HS English teacher jobs in Bothell/Seattle area. Edmonds SD secondary teacher 7-12 openings active on Frontline (applitrack.com/edmonds). No new site-specific Edmonds postings this week. LWSD application started 2026-04-15 — STATUS UNKNOWN: follow up on completion and submission. King's Schools (Shoreline) and Seattle Christian School HS Language Arts listings from April still active per prior session.
career.sps_jobs: SPS Language Arts/Social Studies Teacher Recruitment Pool 2026-27 remains active at seattleschools.org/departments/hr/careers/. Set 'Teacher' job category alert. REMINDER: fix interest-card miscategorization.

family.choir: Seattle Peace Chorus spring 2026 auditions by arrangement. Seattle Pro Musica: August auditions (auditions@seattlepromusica.org). Choral Arts Northwest: July auditions. Seattle Men's/Women's Choruses: September auditions for 2026-27 season.
family.toddler: Silver Creek Indoor PlayPark — Silver Creek Family Church, Lynnwood; Thu May 7, 10am–1pm (first appearance). ChildStrive Together for Families — Lynnwood Event Center; Thu May 7, 11am–1pm, free. Flying Heritage & Combat Armor Museum, Everett — free admission Thu May 7, 10am–4pm (first appearance). Sno-Isle: Toddler Storytime with Heather — Mountlake Terrace branch, Tue May 12.

global_news.findings: Iran reviewing US peace proposal; Trump calls deal 'very possible' — FM Araghchi confirmed Hormuz, nuclear program, and sanctions all on the table; Pakistan expects preliminary accord soon. Rubio in Rome May 7 to meet Pope Leo XIV, easing Vatican-Washington tensions. Germany: 50,000+ pupils striking over rearmament policy. Guardian investigation: UAE ruling royal family benefits from €71M+ in EU farming subsidies. Russia's secret spy school revealed — hacking and election meddling. French cargo ship attacked in Strait of Hormuz despite talks.

French cargo ship Hormuz attack. NYT passes 13 million subscribers. 'Does This Feel Like a Catholic Moment?' — Pope Leo XIV confrontation with Trump. Trump-backed Ramaswamy wins Ohio governor primary. Met Gala 2026 coverage.

Israel air strikes kill 5 in Gaza; son of Hamas negotiator critically wounded. Iran calls US demands a 'wishlist.' Hantavirus cruise ship: ~40 passengers disembarked at St. Helena; ship heading to Canary Islands.

Devils Hole pupfish reach crisis as federal science posts gutted. Border czar Homan: 'Mass deportations are coming.' RFK Jr. at MAHA Summit. PSG advances to Champions League final after beating Bayern Munich.

Iran reviewing US proposal May 7; Pakistan expects deal. Senator Daines meets Chinese FM Wang Yi in Beijing. Rubio arrives at Vatican. Preliminary accord would extend ceasefire plus 30-day broader settlement window.
Ramaswamy wins Ohio governor primary. OpenAI 'io' division building AI agent phone — no apps, mass production 2028, $6.5B Jony Ive acquisition. DOJ struggling to hire lawyers after mass resignations.

intellectual_journals.findings: May 14 issue: 'A Vital Unconscious' (Coco Fusco) — Wifredo Lam's synthesis of European modernism and Afro-Cuban consciousness; La Jungla (1942–43) acquired by MoMA 1945 but hung by coatroom for years. 'Art for Our Age of Chaos' (Jed Perl) — Whitney Biennial 2026 and New Museum 'New Humans: Memories of the Future.' 'This Bitter Earth' (Rosa Lyster) — salt lakes as climate barometers; Aral Sea essentially vanished. 'Drawn to the Void' (Julian Bell) — Joseph Wright of Derby.

May 11-18 issue additional pieces: 'The Idea That Reshaped Identity Politics' — Kimberlé Crenshaw's 1989 intersectionality article and its migration to T-shirts and weaponization. 'The American Revolution Wasn't That Big a Deal' — slavery as correct Civil War lens; Jefferson and Adams both died July 4, 1826. 'Glorious Country' — Frederic Church's art.

May 7 issue: 'Plan A: Economic Warfare' (Jamie Martin) — dollar weaponization from Banco Delta Asia to Iran sanctions; smuggling ring broken up at fake NJ mafia wedding. 'Ask Claude' (Paul Taylor) — 'first profession replaced by AI will be computer programmer'; from FORTRAN to coding with Claude.
May 4: 'Trying Not to Try: Wu-Wei' — Edward Slingerland on perfectionism limiting success; wu-wei paradox: victory in Mindball only by relaxing.

Jon Krakauer on Everest post-Into Thin Air: 'The deadly hazards I wrote about attracted novice climbers.' Every Frame a Painting releasing video essays regularly again. Nolan's The Odyssey trailer released — IMAX epic, July 17.
No new investigation this week. Prior: Pulitzer for predatory towing; Trump midterm takeover; Veterans Affairs mental health staffing crisis.
No new investigation this week. Prior: Sightline AI (Portland) shipping autonomous targeting boards to Israeli drone makers.
May 2026 cover ongoing: chronic inflammation/colchicine; altermagnets; freshwater mussel decline. No new coverage this session.

NEW: 'Trump Is Siccing Shady Private Debt Collectors on Immigrants' — DHS charging $1,000/day for overstaying deportation orders; max fine $1.8M; $6B in fines on 20,000+ immigrants; hearings gutted; new $5,000 border-crossing fine via One Big Beautiful Bill. Attorney Charles Moore: 'part and parcel of the administration's desire to partner with the private sector to make immigration enforcement as punitive and painful as possible.'
No new finds this session. Prior: $10.8B 2026 midterm ad spending projected.

wearable_ai.query: wearable AI pendants pins lifelogging devices 2026
wearable_ai.findings: NEW: OpenAI 'io' division building screenless pocket AI companion — 'no apps, just AI agents.' Mass production 2028. 300-400M units target. $6.5B Jony Ive acquisition. Qualcomm +13%. Most significant wearable AI development of the week — OpenAI's full post-smartphone form-factor commitment.

Xreal (Google's Android XR hardware arm) MWC 2026 prototypes cleared most FCC tests — concrete pre-launch milestone. Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra: world's first Privacy Display on mobile. Walmart Gemini-powered smart speaker expanding to retail partners.

triadic_ontology.findings: No new peer-reviewed papers this week. Synthesis: formal spine (Verlag Alber / Triertium / Raffaelli / Gorsuch / Brown / Bailey / Yamamoto) unchanged. Rome June 2026 ('Nature Lost, Nature Regained') is nearest calendar anchor. Applied resonances: Brothers Karamazov as lived triadic ontology continues; Trump-Pope Leo confrontation (populist nationalism / institutional Catholicism / silenced migrant-defense third term) acquires new arc — Rubio flying to Rome May 7 adds a mediation layer the original triad lacked.

ai_systems.findings: New: ARIS (arXiv 2605.03042) — adversarial autonomous research pipeline: generator LLM drafts, reviewer LLM critiques in loop, surfaces integrity failures missed by single-agent pipelines; ships persistent per-project research wiki. Recursive Multi-Agent Systems (arXiv 2604.25917) — agents instantiating sub-agents recursively. RRTL (arXiv 2505.17106) — red teaming reasoning LLMs in tool learning: disabling tools reduces jailbreaks but degrades performance; safety-capability tradeoff quantified. MLPerf Inference v6.0: 2.5M tokens/s on NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra, 2.7× software optimization over v5.1. MARAUS: multi-agent RAG cuts hallucination from 15% to 1.45% in production.

uap.findings: NEW: AARO announced plans (from Aug 2025 classified workshop with Associated Universities Inc.) to standardize UAP report collection with civilian researchers — first formal civilian research integration into AARO pipeline. Corbell and Knapp confirmed to Congress they have personally viewed most of the 46 withheld UAP videos; provided Congress specific file names and locations including US Air Force materials. Feb 3 mushroom-shaped UAP footage (Syria-Jordan border, instantaneous acceleration) continues in official channels. No new international (UK/France/Japan) government actions this week.

newyorker.title: On the High Line, Buddha Is the New Giant Pigeon
newyorker.text: Before last year, Cecilia Alemani, the chief curator at the High Line, had never heard of—much less seen—a pigeon diaper. "We had an event for National Pigeon Appreciation Day," she said, occasioned by a giant, hyperrealistic statue of the bird, called "Dinosaur." Conceived by the Colombian artist Iván Argote, and unveiled in 2024, the sculpture spent a year and a half staring down commuters from its perch on the Spur, a section of the High Line that looms over the intersection of Thirtieth Street and Tenth Avenue. New Yorkers embraced it as a pop-up mascot; in Alemani's words, "They recognized themselves in this idol hovering at the city's edge." More than five thousand attended the appreciation, donning pigeon drag, pummelling a pigeon piñata, and showing off their pigeon pets, diapers, and accessories. Now the pigeon is gone—replaced by a Buddha. For now, the Buddha's feet were stuck near the top of their support poles; a man in the scissor lift struggled to push them farther apart. Alemani was undaunted. "The city is going to wake up with a new sculpture that looks like it's always been there," she declared, which even the pigeon diehards might learn to love: "Maybe they can think of it as a reincarnation." ♦

enriched_articles.title: Iran Reviewing US Proposal as Trump Pressures Tehran for Agreement to End War
enriched_articles.text: Iran is reviewing a US peace proposal as President Trump predicted a swift end to the conflict, calling a deal 'very possible.' Iran's FM Araghchi confirmed talks covered three core issues: the Strait of Hormuz (Iran's principal leverage), Tehran's disputed nuclear program, and relief from US-led sanctions. Pakistan — ceasefire broker since April 8 — expects a preliminary accord soon: an immediate ceasefire extension and a 30-day window for broader negotiations. A French cargo ship was attacked in the Strait this week even as Tehran reviews the US document. Senator Daines met Chinese FM Wang Yi in Beijing to coordinate diplomatic pressure. Trump demands a 'major rollback' of Iran's nuclear program; Iran calls these demands a 'wishlist.' Separately, Secretary Rubio flew to Rome to meet Pope Leo XIV to ease a parallel diplomatic rupture after Trump criticized the pontiff's stance on the war.

enriched_articles.title: Trump Is Siccing Shady Private Debt Collectors on Immigrants
enriched_articles.text: The Trump administration is fining immigrants nearly $1,000 per day for overstaying deportation orders — including orders issued years ago — and has contracted private debt collectors to pursue the debts. As of last year, an estimated $6 billion in fines had been levied against 20,000+ immigrants. The statute of limitations is five years, making the maximum fine $1.8 million — a sum that cannot be waived unless the immigrant leaves the country. New rules gutted administrative hearings where immigrants could previously contest fines; they now receive only written notice. The administration added a new $5,000 fine for illegal border crossings, authorized by the One Big Beautiful Bill. 'This is part and parcel [of] the administration's desire to partner with the private sector to make immigration enforcement as punitive and painful as possible,' said Charles Moore of Public Justice. Over 20,000 people fined by August 2025 alone.

enriched_articles.title: This Bitter Earth — Rosa Lyster, NYRB May 14, 2026
enriched_articles.text: Rosa Lyster reviews Tracey's book on salt lakes as the 'canary in the coal mine' for the climate crisis — shrinking at a drastic rate worldwide. Salt lakes depend on a delicate balance between inflow and outflow; as the end point of larger water systems, changes thousands of kilometers upstream profoundly affect them. 'When the strange, hidden salt lakes start dying, it means entire ecosystems are in bad shape.' The Aral Sea — once the fourth-largest salt lake in the world — has essentially vanished since the 1960s, destroyed by Soviet cotton irrigation demand, now barely visible on satellite images. Rivers feeding salt lakes have been diverted for decades; climate change accelerates evaporation. Lyster finds the book's personal-memoiristic framing a distraction from its genuinely alarming data — an editor-driven syndrome of demanding 'your feelings' from authors of serious analytical work.
